content:
IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.

If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill using the Skill tool BEFORE any response, any action, any clarifying question.

User instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" does not mean skip workflows. Check for skills FIRST.

## Priority Chain

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md contradicts a skill, follow CLAUDE.md. The user is in control.

## Anti-Rationalization

These thoughts mean STOP — you are rationalizing skipping a skill check:

| Thought                              | Reality                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| "This is simple enough"              | Simple tasks have skills too. Check.                                           |
| "I already know how"                 | Skills contain project-specific overrides you don't have memorized. Invoke it. |
| "Let me explore first"               | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first.                                   |
| "I need more context"                | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. Always.                         |
| "The skill is overkill"              | Simple things become complex. Use it.                                          |
| "I'll just do this one thing first"  | Check BEFORE doing anything. No exceptions.                                    |
| "I remember this skill"              | Skills evolve. Read the current version via the Skill tool.                    |
| "This doesn't need a formal process" | If a skill exists for it, use it. Period.                                      |
| "Let me look at the code quickly"    | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills.                             |
| "This feels productive"              | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this.                         |
| "This is just a discussion"          | Questions about features, bugs, or architecture ARE development tasks. Check.  |

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, commit format, verification): Follow exactly. Do not adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (React patterns, styling, architecture): Adapt principles to context. The spirit matters more than the letter.

The skill itself tells you which type it is. When unclear, treat as rigid.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorm, start, planning, debugging) — these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Domain skills second** (react, typescript, tanstack) — these guide execution
3. **Workflow skills third** (commit, verification, close) — these govern shipping

## Subagent Exemption

If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific, scoped task — skip this enforcement. Subagents follow their prompt, not the full skill ceremony.

content.text:
<skill name="imf-notes-timesheet" location="/Users/brunner/repos/nikbrunner/imf-notes/.agents/skills/imf-notes-timesheet/SKILL.md">
References are relative to /Users/brunner/repos/nikbrunner/imf-notes/.agents/skills/imf-notes-timesheet.

# imf-notes:timesheet

**I AM A TIMESHEET GENERATOR. WHEN LOADED, I EXECUTE IMMEDIATELY.**

Do not ask for confirmation. Do not say "I am ready" or any variant. Do not analyze the user's intent. Do not wait for follow-up. The skill invocation IS the instruction.

Parse the argument, gather all data sources, format the output, write to the daily note, and respond — without pausing.

Argument rules: none → today. `yesterday` → yesterday. `YYYY-MM-DD` → that date.

This is rigid. Execute now.

---

## Step 1 — Resolve the date and path

### 1a — Determine target DATE (YYYY-MM-DD)

Check the argument passed with the skill invocation:
- **No argument** → today. `date '+%Y-%m-%d'`
- **Argument is `yesterday`** → yesterday. `date -v-1d '+%Y-%m-%d'`
- **Argument is `YYYY-MM-DD`** → use it directly.

### 1b — Build daily note path from DATE

```bash
year="${DATE:0:4}"
month="${DATE:5:2}"
padded="${year}.${month}.${DATE:8:2}"
dayname=$(date -j -f '%Y-%m-%d' "$DATE" '+%a')
VAULT="$HOME/repos/nikbrunner/imf-notes"
DAILY_PATH="$VAULT/logs/${year}/${year}.${month}/${padded} - ${dayname}.md"
```

### 1c — Validate

Check file exists: `test -f "$DAILY_PATH"`. If not, note it and continue with empty tasks.

### 1d — Compute NEXT_DATE for git queries

```bash
NEXT_DATE=$(date -j -v+1d -f '%Y-%m-%d' "$DATE" '+%Y-%m-%d')
```

## Step 2 — Read the daily note

Read the daily note at the resolved path.

Extract only completed tasks (`[x]` and `[~]`) from the top-level section only — skip `## Optional` and any sub-headings.

Filter out one-word tasks ("Mails"), reminders ("Timesheet"), and meta-process tasks. When in doubt, include it.

## Step 3 — Gather git commits

Run this for each repo under `~/repos/imfusion/` (web-sdk, web-ui, web-ui-poc, web-viewer, web-viewer-next):

```
git -C ~/repos/imfusion/$REPO log --author="brunner@imfusion.com" \
  --since="$DATE 00:00" --until="$NEXT_DATE 00:00" \
  --format="%h %s"
```

Collect all commits regardless of branch.

## Step 4 — Read ROADMAP.md (if active)

Read `~/repos/imfusion/web-ui-poc/ROADMAP.md`. Extract all `[x]` and `[~]` items from the dated section matching the target date.

After gathering web-ui-poc commits, cross-reference against the roadmap's `[x]` items. If commits exist for work not checked off in the roadmap, note which items are stale.

## Step 5 — Format the prose

Use these transformation rules:

1. **Maximum 3 sentences.** Condense all work into at most 3 sentences. Each sentence will contain multiple items folded together.

2. **Sort by impact, not chronology.** The first sentence is the most important work of the day. The last sentence is the least important. Ticket/key feature work comes before styling, refactoring, chores.

3. **One sentence per line.** No mid-sentence line breaks.

4. Strip `- [x]`, `- [~]`, `- ` prefixes. Output as sentences with trailing period.

5. Past tense: "Write X" → "Wrote X", "Set up X" / "Setup X" → "Set up X", "Update X" → "Updated X", "Progress on X" → "Made progress on X" or "Worked on X", no verb → "Worked on X".

6. Flatten wiki-links: `[[Mattia]]` → `Mattia`.

7. Simplify Jira links: `[[WEBSDK-135] Title - Jira](url)` → `WEBSDK-135: Title`. Drop Bitbucket URLs, keep link text.

8. Fold subtasks inline in parentheses: "Migrate from [[web-viewer-tng]]" → `(migrated from web-viewer-tng)`.

9. Convert conventional commits to prose: `feat(x): y` → "Added y in x", `fix(x): y` → "Fixed y in x", `refactor(x): y` → "Refactored y in x", `docs: y` → "Updated documentation: y", `wip(x): y` → "Worked on y in x".

10. Deduplicate: if a commit or roadmap item maps to a daily note task (same ticket key/keywords), fold detail into that task's sentence. Unmatched items become standalone entries. Don't repeat the same work across multiple sentences.

11. Don't invent detail. When in doubt, keep it terse.

## Step 6 — Write to daily note

Upsert the prose under a `# Timesheet` heading:

- If `# Timesheet` already exists, replace everything after it (keep the heading, replace the body).
- If not, append `\n\n# Timesheet\n\n<prose>` to the end.

Do NOT use `obsidian daily:append` — use direct file read/write. Obsidian CLI is only for path resolution.

## Step 7 — Respond

**Your entire response must be exactly this: a code fence (no language tag) containing the formatted prose. Nothing else. No preamble. No markdown outside the fence.**

If the roadmap is stale, append a warning after the code block: `⚠️ Roadmap stale: X, Y, Z committed but not checked off.`

### Example

Daily tasks:
```
- [x] Write [[Mattia]] about proposed Meeting structure
- [x] Setup Claude Code
- [x] [[WEBSDK-135] UI Research & Mockups - Jira](https://...)
  - Migrate from [[web-viewer-tng]]
- [~] Progress on [WEBSDK-140 Web UI PoC - Jira](https://...)
```

Commits (web-ui-poc):
```
feat(explorer): use CodeBlock in GeneratedCssPanel
feat(explorer): center shell body on wide viewports
refactor: rename playground to explorer
```

Your entire response — nothing else (3 sentences, by impact):
```
Made progress on WEBSDK-140: Web UI PoC, refactoring the playground to an explorer with a centered shell body and CodeBlock in GeneratedCssPanel. Continued WEBSDK-135: UI Research & Mockups (migrated from web-viewer-tng). Set up Claude Code and wrote Mattia about proposed meeting structure.
```

Stale warning (if applicable):
```
⚠️ Roadmap stale: cx unit tests, useMediaQuery hook committed but not checked off.
```
</skill>

logs/2026/2026.05/2026.05.22 - Fri.md

rerun the skill for this date please

content.thinking:
The user wants me to rerun the imf-notes-timesheet skill for the date 2026-05-22 (Friday). According to the skill, I should execute immediately without asking for confirmation.

Let me follow the steps:

1. Resolve date: 2026-05-22
2. Build path: year=2026, month=05, padded=2026.05.22, dayname=Fri
   DAILY_PATH = "$VAULT/logs/2026/2026.05/2026.05.22 - Fri.md"
3. Validate the file exists
4. Compute NEXT_DATE = 2026-05-23
5. Read the daily note
6. Gather git commits from imfusion repos
7. Read ROADMAP.md
8. Format prose
9. Write to daily note
10. Respond with exactly the code fence containing the formatted prose

Let me start by checking if the file exists, reading it, and gathering git commits in parallel.

arguments.command:
VAULT="$HOME/repos/nikbrunner/imf-notes"
DAILY_PATH="$VAULT/logs/2026/2026.05/2026.05.22 - Fri.md"
test -f "$DAILY_PATH" && echo "EXISTS" || echo "NOT FOUND"
echo "$DAILY_PATH"

content.thinking: Good, the file exists. Now let me read the daily note, gather git commits from all imfusion repos, and read ROADMAP.md in parallel.

arguments.command:
DATE="2026-05-22"
NEXT_DATE="2026-05-23"
for REPO in web-sdk web-ui web-ui-poc web-viewer web-viewer-next; do
  echo "=== $REPO ==="
  git -C ~/repos/imfusion/$REPO log --author="brunner@imfusion.com" \
    --since="$DATE 00:00" --until="$NEXT_DATE 00:00" \
    --format="%h %s" 2>/dev/null || echo "(repo not found or error)"
  echo ""
done

#003FED

# Timesheet

Extended the component system with ComponentMeta description and origin tracking, Doc.Origin badges, ChipLink primitive, a Buttons category, and a status/primary token rename.

Overhauled shadow infrastructure — dedicated shadow tokens file, parametric direction via a sundial angle convention, and Shadow Strength + Sun Angle knobs in the explorer.

Polished explorer layout with sticky page headers, shell border and shadow fixes, a Typo.Detail size token, and PRDs for color token rework and knob-panel swatches.

# ROADMAP **Legend:** - `[x]` — Done - `[~]` — Partially done / WIP - `[ ]` — Not started ## Scope What this PoC needs to be considered complete. ### Required for PoC - [x] Monorepo setup - [x] Library package - [x] `Web UI Provider` - [x] Architecture documentation - [x] Design token vocabulary - [x] Basic Playground app - [x] Pre-commit hooks - [x] Linting accross all packages - [x] Vitest unit tests - [x] User ready docs/ folder - [x] Component primitives - [x] Web UI Explorer with Docs (via MDX) and Config and Knob Panel - see [PRD](./prds/archive/mdx-doc-schema.md) - [x] Controlled Preview component — see [PRD](./prds/archive/controlled-preview.md) - [x] Configurable colors - [ ] CVA modifier convention — replaces `data-*` per-modifier pattern. Needs PRD. - [ ] React 19 peer dependency — eliminates `forwardRef` - [ ] SSR compatibility — audit hooks/DOM access, document stance - [ ] Testing pattern — smoke contract + `*.test.tsx` scaffolded by `imf-web-ui-new-component` - [ ] LLM accessibility — `llms.txt` + per-component frontmatter - [ ] One absorbed component — `Row` or `Table` (proves pattern beyond Base UI adapters) - [ ] Verify proper base styling - [ ] Bundle strategy research — PRD only; implementation deferred to Production ### Production - [ ] Hosting of Web UI Explorer - [ ] Release flow - [ ] Storybook — evaluate against Explorer; add only if it offers something Explorer can't - [ ] Visual regression tests — Playwright + Bitbucket Pipelines workflow - [ ] Searchable docs — see [PRD](./prds/searchable-docs.md) - [ ] Configurable logo - [ ] Consumer config typegen - [ ] Add a real, empty playground app for end-user testing - [ ] Add basic hooks (iterative) - [ ] LLM accessibility — MCP server - [ ] Bundle strategy — implementation ### Extras ### Component Primitives See [here](./packages/web-ui/src/components) ## Open Questions - Reduce prop re-maps for initial version? (at least for the `base-ui` components) ## Open Tasks - [ ] `web-ui`: Export `useDisclosure` hook (app-shell.tsx uses inline useState booleans for nav/knobs open state) - [ ] `web-ui`: Make sure every Web UI Element Props gets extended by their base HTML attributes - [ ] `web-ui/catalog`: Fine tune default values for packages/web-ui/src/knobs/catalog.ts - [ ] `explorer`: Knob Values Values in URL - [ ] `explorer`: Selectable Themes - [ ] `web-ui`: Resolved properties types for generated PropsTables (e.g. `SwipeDirection` => `"up" | "down" | "left" | "right"`) - Base UI's implementation - [ ] `web-ui/docs`: Split `architecture.md` into focused smaller files (one per concern); absorb content from `component-definition.md` and `conventions.md` where there's overlap; make each file independently readable without cross-references - [ ] `explorer`: Migrate remaining relative imports in `apps/explorer/src/` to use `@explorer/components` and `@explorer/styles` aliases (routes/\_\_root.tsx, routes/index.tsx, dashboard-card, shell-nav, etc.) - [ ] `web-ui`: Publish our own LLM-friendly docs for absorbed / pure-imf primitives (Chip, Stack, Typo, …) — mirror Base UI's convention (`/page` + `/page.md`) so each component can populate `docLink` + `llmDocLink` even without an upstream package ## LOG ### 2026.05.25 - Mon - [ ] `explorer`: Introduce dedicated "Examples" section (Gives us real examples in type-safe way - Maybe even point from the - [ ] `web-ui`: Configurable border radius — define a base radius token; round/less-round values derive from it so components scale together (relates to `Chip` inline mode using `radius-3`) - [ ] `web-ui`: Add `Row` component (counter to `Stack`) — replaces `apps/explorer/src/components/layout/row/row.tsx` (waits on modifier convention) - [ ] `web-ui`: Add `Table` component — replaces `apps/explorer/src/components/doc/prop-table/prop-table.tsx` (waits on modifier convention) ### 2026.05.22 - Fri - [x] `web-ui`: Adjust primary color to the new brand color - [x] `web-ui/shadows`: Move shadow tokens to dedicated `variables/shadows.css` - [x] `web-ui/shadows`: Parametric shadow direction via `--imf-ui-shadow-angle` (sundial convention; 0° = sun overhead, clockwise) - [x] `explorer/elevation`: Couple elevation mixin to the shared shadow angle (default 315°) - [x] `web-ui/knobs`: Open knob namespace beyond `color-` prefix (`cssVarPrefix` shift; existing color knobs gain `color-` segment) - [x] `web-ui/knobs`: Add `Sun Angle` + `Shadow Strength` knobs (new `shadow` group; elevation loosely coupled to strength via 2.5% dampening) - [x] `web-ui/catalog`: `primary` own group + direct color tokens — own token-architecture note - [x] `web-ui`: Extend `ComponentMeta` — `description` (LLM-discoverable identity), `origin` (`adapted` | `native`), nested `upstream` (`name` / `websiteLink` / `docLink` / `llmDocLink`); architecture rule 11 + PRD + glossary aligned; scaffold skill updated - [x] `refactor(docs)`: Better name for `apps/explorer/docs/component-page.md` - [x] `explorer`: `Doc.Origin` component — origin badge (Base UI logo / Sparkles + label) plus link chips; replaces `Doc.DocLinks`; page header now rendered from meta (description + origin block) - [x] `web-ui`: Add `Buttons` category; relocate `Button` from `Inputs` - [x] `web-ui`: Add `ChipLink` primitive in Buttons — anchor-styled chip with `kind` prop (external / internal) and auto-appended Lucide `ArrowUpRight` / `ArrowRight`. Reuses Chip CVA via extracted `chip.cva.ts` - [x] Make component page headers sticky component's doc page to the examples page) ### 2026.05.21 - Thu - [x] Planning & Meeting with myself - [x] Assort ROADMAP and clear Inbox - Install Lucide (or similar) icon library — replace ☰ / ⚙ unicode icons - Extract dashboard page styles into components (DashboardHero, DashboardCard) instead of flat `index.css` - Migrate to CSS Modules for consistency with web-ui (no FOUC risk in pure CSR; deferred) -
